definition: A high-call-count loop body generates a flood of (call-context, edge) pairs that all look novel to the context-indexed map, draining the mutation budget into context-redundant corpus entries and starving the byte-level exploration needed to satisfy a downstream exit or continuation condition.

Context-sensitive coverage splits the edge-map reward for a sparse, exact-value gate across many call-context slots, diluting the feedback signal that should steer mutation toward the required literal byte or codepoint; the corpus fills with context-duplicate seeds that never carry the needed value.
